epigraph.quote: a beautiful, interesting walk with a goat cheese crepe half way round

per_stop.quote: The sea would have once come up the valley. It's called Montreuil - sur - Mer.
The cathedral windows glowed in the sunshine
Simon paddled in the lake, Eve swam & the girls watched. A long, sandy Beach.
The girls liked the Chaource cheese & Prawn.
She asked Melody & Charlotte if they could hear the grasshoppers
We are in a grove of pines + cork oak. The lower trunk bark has been peeled away. There's a distant view of St. Tropez
The amazing old baptistery at the cathedral, for immersion of adults in the floor
Simon was nervous of the man - florid & big-tummied, who stood next to us swilling beer & wondering how to break into the van.
I was nearly caught photographing too close (resting my hand on the cabinet) by the guards with walkie talkies.
We are next to 2 town drunks in a low tent; v. beaten-up face, chain smoking, slumped against a tree
Cuneo is still nice - "style" Simon said, but dignified.
Police arrived at our breakfast table. Once they found out we were English, they were friendly
We've parked over a beautiful view.
The campsite is really lovely, perched high on a valley side, so high that planes fly along the valley below us.
chicken & potatoes lashed in cream, 2 cheeses, apricot flan, coffee & chocolate & red wine
we carried stools into the square & watched the painted facade of Amiens cathedral. It was marvellous, lit up like a jewel, or like an Indian temple.
